payload.answer: Do not commit the full 8000-word transcript as a conversation_summary memory card. It should be compressed into a small set of durable, high-signal facts if anything is retained at all.
citations: Input: proposed one conversation_summary card containing an entire 8000 word transcript
Policy: be strict on committing durable memory and preserve ambiguity

entity_resolution.reason: The proposed card would store an entire transcript as a durable memory, which is too verbose, low-signal, and not appropriately distilled for long-term memory.

details: Rejected the proposed transcript-sized memory card.

repair_options: Extract only a few stable, high-signal takeaways instead of the full transcript.
If no durable facts are present, store nothing.
If a summary is needed, reduce it to brief, non-redundant bullet-like memory cards with clear confidence levels.
